_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Empyema/ : Empyema_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Empyema_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists).
min_doctor_qualification: DNB / MD/MS (General Medicine / Pediatric Medicine / General surgery / Thoracic surgery)
clinical_indications: Presence of frank pus in the pleural space (defined as empyema)
Unilateral, markedly asymmetric pleural effusion with blunting of the costophrenic angle on chest imaging

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Chest X ray/Ultrasound chest/ CECT chest report

claim.label: Detailed Indoor clinical papers with treatment details
Post treatment Chest X ray report/Drainage under Ultrasound guidance/Intercostal tube drainage details (If applicable)

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the complete hemogram report submitted?
Was the Chest X ray/Ultrasound chest/ CECT chest report submitted?
Was the clinical notes and chest X ray/Ultrasound chest/CECT, CT Chest indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Was Detailed Indoor case papers with vital (BP and Pulse) and Treatment details submitted?
Was the post treatment Chest X ray report/Drainage under Ultrasound guidance/Intercostal tube drainage details (If applicable) submitted?
Was the Detailed Discharge Summary submitted with the date of the follow-up mentioned?

common_queries: Clinical notes or imaging reports (Chest X ray/Ultrasound chest/CECT, CT Chest) not sufficiently indicative of Empyema for the procedure.
Hemogram report missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Clinical notes at pre-authorization lacking evaluation findings, clear indications for the procedure, or planned line of treatment.
Detailed Indoor clinical papers missing vital signs (BP and Pulse) or complete treatment details at claim submission.
Post-treatment imaging (Chest X-ray) or drainage details (Ultrasound guidance/Intercostal tube drainage) not provided at claim submission.
Discharge summary missing or lacking mentioned follow-up date at claim submission.

additional_information.referral_criteria: Surgical consultation should be a consideration when drainage via tube thoracostomy fails or in multi-loculated empyema.
